fields.summary: [DMS MCP]  MCP Server implementation

issuetype.description: Created by Jira Software - do not edit or delete. Issue type for a user story.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.customfield_12004:
h2. Acceptance Criteria
h3. AC1: MCP Endpoint Accessibility
 * [ ] POST /dms/mcp-service/mcp is reachable on each deployed environment (dev, qa)

 * [ ] The endpoint responds to MCP initialize request with server info (name: dms-mcp-service, version: 0.0.1)

 * [ ] Capability negotiation returns tool: true and resource: false, prompt: false, completion: false

 * [ ] Mcp-Session-Id header is present in the response

h3. AC2: Authentication and Authorization
 * [ ] Requests without a Bearer token receive 401 Unauthorized

 * [ ] Requests with an invalid/expired Bearer token receive 401 Unauthorized

 * [ ] Requests without x-user-jwt header receive 400 with code MISSING_USER_JWT

 * [ ] Requests with malformed x-user-jwt (not 3 dot-separated parts) receive 400 with code INVALID_USER_JWT
 * [ ] Authenticated requests without dms_mcp_tools permission receive 403 with code ACCESS_DENIED and message Insufficient permissions for MCP access (cover the scenarios with the all the permissions or without any persmissions)

 * -[ ] Authenticated requests without dms_mcp_tools READ permission receive 403 with code ACCESS_DENIED and message Insufficient permissions for MCP access-

 * -[ ] Users with READ permission can invoke read-only tools (getOrganization, requestAssessment, getDevicesByPackageId, getSystemUpdatePackages, getBulkScheduleDeployStatus, getPackageTestingByPackageId, getBulkTestingStatus)-

 * -[ ] Users without EXECUTE permission are denied access to bulkTesting, scheduleBulkTesting, markPackages, markOperation-

 * -[ ] Users without UPDATE permission are denied access to bulkDeploy, scheduleBulkDeploy-

h3. AC3: Organization Tools
 * [ ] getOrganization returns organization details (orgId, timestamps, DOS/TSFA flags) for the authenticated user's org

 * [ ] The tool correctly proxies to dms-device-management-service using the x-user-jwt token

h3. AC4: System Update Tools
 * [ ] requestAssessment returns a map of assessment counts

 * [ ] getSystemUpdatePackages supports pagination (pageNumber, pageSize) and optional filters (testStates, search) and returns paginated results

 * [ ] getDevicesByPackageId accepts packageId, pagination parameters, and excludeDeployed flag; returns device list

 * [ ] bulkDeploy accepts requestId and deployments list; returns BulkDeployResponse with deployment results

 * [ ] scheduleBulkDeploy accepts requestId, deployments, and scheduledDateTime; returns confirmation

 * [ ] getBulkScheduleDeployStatus accepts requestId; returns schedule status details

h3. AC5: Package Testing Tools
 * [ ] bulkTesting accepts testDetails, testEndDate, batchId; returns confirmation

 * [ ] scheduleBulkTesting accepts same parameters plus scheduledDateTime; returns confirmation

 * [ ] markPackages accepts allowed boolean and packageIds list; returns confirmation

 * [ ] markOperation accepts allowed boolean and packageTestingId; returns confirmation

 * [ ] getPackageTestingByPackageId accepts packageId; returns SystemUpdateTestingResponse

 * [ ] getBulkTestingStatus accepts batchId; returns batch testing status

h3. AC6: Error Handling
 * [ ] When DMS API returns an error, the tool response includes a meaningful error message (not a raw stack trace)

 * -[ ] Error messages from DMS are truncated to 200 characters maximum-

 * -[ ] Connection timeouts to DMS API (10s connect, 30s read/write) produce a descriptive error-

 * -[ ] Failed requests to DMS API are retried up to 2 times with 500ms delay-

h3. AC7: MCP Inspector Compatibility
 * [ ] All 13 tools are listed when calling tools/list via MCP protocol

 * [ ] Each tool has a description and parameter schema (via @Tool / @ToolParam annotations)

 * [ ] Tools can be invoked from any standard MCP inspector (e.g., MCP Inspector, Claude Desktop, or similar client)

 * [ ] The HTTP-Streamable transport works end-to-end (session creation, tool calls, session termination: when session is deleted we should not be able to access it next time)

h3. AC8: Deployment and Infrastructure
 * [ ] The service is running and healthy on: dms-dev, dms-qa,

 * [ ] -Health endpoint accessible at /dms/mcp-service/actuator/health on port 9000-

 * -[ ] Ambassador routing resolves https://<env-host>/dms/mcp-service/mcp to the service-

 * [ ] -CORS headers allow x-user-jwt, Mcp-Session-Id, Mcp-Protocol-Version in requests-

 
h3. AC9: Environment-Specific Connectivity
 * [ ] dms-dev: api.naea1.uds-dev.lenovo.com/dms/mcp-service/mcp routes correctly; DMS proxy points to dev DMS API

 * [ ] dms-qa: api.naea1.uds-qa.lenovo.com/dms/mcp-service/mcp routes correctly; DMS proxy points to qa DMS API

fields.summary: [Chat Enhancement] Implement follow-up questions for ambiguous queries

issuetype.description: Created by Jira Software - do not edit or delete. Issue type for a user story.
issuetype.name: Story

fields.customfield_12200: As a user, when my question is unclear, the system asks a relevant follow-up question and provides a final answer once my intent is clarified.
fields.customfield_12003:
||*Given*||*When*||*Then*||
|A user submits a query and the system identifies it as low confidence (ambiguous or uncertain)|The system processes the query|The system not just returns a final answer but also displays a contextual follow-up question in the same chat thread|
|A follow-up question is already shown to the user|The user provides the updated prompt|The system proceeds using the clarified intent and returns a response within the same thread|
|A user submits a query and the system identifies it as high confidence|The system processes the query|The system returns a final answer directly without showing any follow-up questions|
|The query is handled by the existing chat orchestration|A follow-up is triggered or resolved|The interaction remains within the same thread and follows standard chat behavior|

fields.summary: Post-Ingestion evaluation for data ingestion pipeline

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.customfield_10400:
|| Given || When || Then ||
| The KB and capabilities ingestion pipeline is triggered | The ingestion run succeeds | RAGAS testing is triggered and the final score is checked. If the score does not pass, the database is rolled back |
| A specific language is provided as a parameter to the ingestion pipeline | The ingestion run is triggered | Only the KB and capabilities for the selected language are ingested without affecting other language data |
| The RAGAS testing flag is enabled or disabled before triggering the ingestion pipeline | The ingestion run succeeds | RAGAS testing is triggered if the flag is enabled and the score is checked. If the score does not pass, the database is rolled back. If the flag is disabled, the pipeline completes without running RAGAS testing |

||Given||When||Then||
|Given a data insight prompt to show a table of data|when the formatting code generation is executed|the code generation agent should not fail for common failures* and create the correct code in first attempt|

*The common failures are listed in description
